summary: The UI change looks internally consistent, but the new e2e spec does not actually prove the highest-risk acceptance path: the mobile Browser tab never mounts a real iframe, so the claimed no-remount / preserved-handshake behavior is untested.

findings.title: Responsive e2e is a false positive for the iframe-persistence requirement
findings.recommendation: Make the stub provide a real browser target and a non-null `/tailscale-ip`, then assert that switching `Claude -> Browser -> Claude -> Browser` preserves the same iframe node/src or at least avoids a second browser-tab/open/attach cycle. As written, `/browser-target` is forced to 404 and `/tailscale-ip` is `{ip:null}`, so `BrowserPane` can only render its empty/error shell; the later `.app-pane` visibility check therefore proves only that the wrapper is shown, not that the iframe mounted or survived tab switches.
